message:
fix: stabilize mux Cx-cancellation test, feature-gate channel mutability, fix proptest refs

Three independent fixes addressing test flakiness, compilation under
asupersync, and proptest macro compatibility:

mux_client.rs (send_request_only_with_precancelled_cx test):
- Add handshake synchronization barrier using std::sync::mpsc::channel.
  Previously the test had a race condition: the client could attempt the
  pre-cancelled ListPanes request before the mock server finished
  processing the codec/client-ID handshake, causing the server to see
  the ListPanes as part of the handshake flow and miss the cancellation
  assertion. Now the server signals via a channel when handshake is
  complete, and the client waits (with 2s timeout) before proceeding.
- Increase mock server read timeout from 200ms to 1000ms to prevent
  spurious timeouts on loaded CI machines.

runtime.rs:
- Feature-gate `mut` on stream_exit_rx channel receiver. Under
  asupersync-runtime, the channel's recv() takes &self (not &mut self),
  so the binding must not be `mut` to avoid an unused_mut warning. Split
  into two cfg branches: asupersync-runtime gets `let (tx, rx)` while
  the tokio path keeps `let (tx, mut rx)`.

proptest_pool.rs:
- Change prop_assert_ne!(timeout, closed) to prop_assert_ne!(&timeout,
  &closed) (and same for the other two pairs). The proptest macro needs
  references when the type doesn't implement Copy — PoolError has Clone
  but not Copy, so passing by value moved the values and prevented
  subsequent comparisons.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
